content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Dan          Natalie     John G.      Bob G.         Date : January 3, 1977
    Mickey        John K.     Jay          Barbara
    Ken F.✓       Rich E.     Bob          Ken G.
    Mike          Gina        Fred         Del
    Ted           Dawn        Richard      Alfredo
                                                          M - 77-587

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION - Ed Kerry

Subject: Policy Interpretation of New Government Code §87206.5


     Frequently, particularly in rural areas, a designated
employee's principal residence is located on a substantial
parcel of property, whether 10, 100, or 1,000 acres. When
real property is required to be disclosed by the person
pursuant to a Conflict of Interest Code, what, if any,
portion of this real property is required to be disclosed
in light of new Government Code §87206.5, which provides:

     "For purposes of this article 'interest
     in real property' does not include the
     principal residence of the filer."?

     At the opinion request meeting on December 30, 1976,
it was agreed that in the usual residential context, this
Code section excludes the principal residence itself and
the lot or real property on which the principal residence
is located, so long as the property does not constitute
any other type of reportable interest, such as a report-
able investment or reportable income. When the real
property consists of land in addition to the portion on
which the principal residence sits, all such additional
property is disclosable.


EK:gb

sections.conclusion: In the usual residential situation, Government Code §87206.5 excludes the principal residence itself and the lot or real property on which the principal residence is located from disclosure, so long as the property does not constitute any other type of reportable interest such as a reportable investment or reportable income. When the real property consists of land in addition to the lot on which the principal residence sits, all such additional property is disclosable.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a filer's principal residence and the lot on which it is located must be disclosed under new Government Code §87206.5, which exempts principal residences from real property disclosure requirements.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that a principal residence and its lot are excluded from disclosure under Government Code §87206.5, but any additional real property beyond the residential lot must be disclosed if it would otherwise be reportable.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is internal FPPC memorandum with distribution list rather than traditional letter format. No formal 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' headers present, but content provides clear policy interpretation. Image quality affects confidence - some text is unclear or partially obscured. Parsed as advice letter based on FPPC providing interpretive guidance.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a filer's principal residence and the lot on which it is located must be disclosed under new Government Code §87206.5, which exempts principal residences from real property disclosure requirements.

CONCLUSION: In the usual residential situation, Government Code §87206.5 excludes the principal residence itself and the lot or real property on which the principal residence is located from disclosure, so long as the property does not constitute any other type of reportable interest such as a reportable investment or reportable income. When the real property consists of land in addition to the lot on which the principal residence sits, all such additional property is disclosable.

When real property is required to be disclosed by the person pursuant to a Conflict of Interest Code, what, if any, portion of this real property is required to be disclosed in light of new Government Code §87206.5, which provides: "For purposes of this article 'interest in real property' does not include the principal residence of the filer."? At the opinion request meeting on December 30, 1976, it was agreed that in the usual residential context, this Code section excludes the principal residence itself and the lot or real property on which the principal residence is located, so long as the property does not constitute any other type of reportable interest, such as a report- able investment or reportable income. When the real property consists of land in addition to the portion on which the principal residence sits, all such additional property is disclosable. EK:gb
embedding.summary: FPPC memorandum providing policy interpretation of Government Code §87206.5 regarding which real property interests must be disclosed, clarifying that principal residences and their lots are excluded from disclosure requirements.
